title: Brownfield Capacity-Constrained Sprint Plan
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-task-list.md`, `./access-review-modernization-estimate-set.md`, `./access-review-modernization-risk-register.md`, and `./access-review-modernization-capacity-note.md`. Work only from those files. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue. Produce a sprint-planning artifact for the next iteration that starts from actual capacity, keeps risky and dependency-heavy work visible, separates committed work from stretch work, and avoids turning the output into a generic roadmap or backlog priority list.

assertions.name: capacity-first
assertions.description: Starts from the supplied team capacity and carry-over constraints instead of desired scope

assertions.name: commitment-is-bounded
assertions.description: Commits only the smallest defensible set of work that fits the sprint goal

assertions.name: dependencies-shape-sequence
assertions.description: Orders work according to dependencies and risk pressure instead of flattening everything into a priority list

assertions.name: stretch-is-explicit
assertions.description: Separates committed work from stretch and deferred work clearly

assertions.name: does-not-turn-into-roadmap
assertions.description: Stays at iteration-planning level rather than becoming a generic long-range roadmap
